## ECIP Submission

Thank you for submitting an Ethereum Classic Improvement Proposal!

Please place your ECIP Markdown file in the `_specs/` directory (e.g. `_specs/ecip-johndoe-myproposal.md`) using `ecip-X.md` as your starting template. An ECIP editor will assign a final number before merging.

---

### Front-Matter Checklist

Verify that your ECIP file's front-matter block contains all required fields per [ECIP-1000](_specs/ecip-1000.md):

- [ ] `ecip:` — ECIP number, or `?` before one is assigned
- [ ] `title:` — Short, descriptive title (≤ 44 characters recommended)
- [ ] `lang:` — Language code (e.g. `en`)
- [ ] `author:` — Real name(s) and optionally email address or GitHub handle
- [ ] `discussions-to:` — URL of the public discussion thread (Discord, GitHub issue, etc.)
- [ ] `status:` — One of: `WIP` · `Draft` · `Last Call` · `Accepted` · `Final` · `Deferred` · `Replaced` · `Rejected` · `Withdrawn` · `Active`
- [ ] `type:` — One of: `Standards Track` · `Meta` · `Informational`
- [ ] `category:` — *(required when `type: Standards Track`)* One of: `Core` · `Networking` · `Interface` · `ECBP`
- [ ] `created:` — Date in ISO 8601 format (`YYYY-MM-DD`)

### Section Checklist

Verify that your ECIP document contains all required sections:

- [ ] `### Abstract` — Short (~200 word) description of the technical issue
- [ ] `### Motivation` — Why the existing protocol is inadequate
- [ ] `### Specification` — Precise technical specification of the proposed change
- [ ] `### Rationale` — Design decisions, alternatives considered, and community consensus
- [ ] `### Implementation` — Reference implementation or notes (may be added before `Final` status)
- [ ] `### Copyright/Licensing` — Explicit license terms (see ECIP-1000 for accepted licenses)

---

### Description

<!-- Briefly describe what this ECIP proposes and why it is needed. -->
